originalTitle: Partner of US influencer who died in Zanzibar speaking to police as witness
title: News from Zanzibar: Let's Talk About Sad Feelings and Helping Each Other
content:
Hello from Your Kid News Reporter!

Hi everyone! It's me, your friendly Kid News Reporter for Africa! I'm here to tell you about some important news today. Sometimes, news can be a little bit sad, but it's always important to learn and understand. We will talk about it in a kind and gentle way. We'll also learn about how we can help each other when things are tough. This story comes from a beautiful place called Zanzibar. Zanzibar is a lovely island not far from the big country of Tanzania. It has sunny beaches and kind people.

A Sad Story in Zanzibar

We heard some news about a lady named Ashly Robinson. She was known online as Ashlee Jenae. Ashly was a grown-up who liked to share happy things on the internet. Many people knew her from her fun videos and pictures. But Ashly became very, very sad. Sometimes, grown-ups and children can feel a big sadness inside. It's like a heavy cloud that stays with them. Ashly was feeling this big sadness. Sadly, Ashly passed away. This means she is no longer with us. When someone is very, very sad and unwell, sometimes their body and mind stop working. This makes many people who knew her feel very sad too. The grown-ups who are police officers are trying to understand everything that happened. They are talking to people who knew Ashly. They want to make sure they know the whole story.

Who Was Ashly Robinson?

Ashly Robinson was what we call an “influencer.” Do you know what that means? It means she liked to share her life with many friends online. She would post pictures and videos. She showed people fun things she was doing. Maybe she was trying a new recipe, or showing a cool place she visited. People liked to watch her videos and learn from her. It was like she had many, many friends all over the world! It is very kind to share joy and smiles with others. Ashly tried to do that a lot. But even people who seem happy online can sometimes have big feelings of sadness inside. **Did you know?** An “influencer” is someone who can make many people want to try new things, like a new game or a new snack, just by showing it online!

Why Do People Get Very Sad?

It's important to remember that all of us have feelings. We can feel happy, silly, excited, and sometimes, we can feel sad or worried. These feelings are like colours in a rainbow; they are all part of us. Sometimes, feelings can become very, very big. So big that they make us feel unwell. When someone feels this big sadness for a long time, it can make them very, very tired. This is called feeling "unwell" in their mind, or sometimes we call it mental health. It's not anyone's fault if they feel this way. It's like when you get a cold or a fever. You don't choose to get sick. Sometimes our minds need care and help, just like our bodies do. **Did you know?** There are special grown-ups, like doctors for your feelings, who can help people who feel very sad or worried. They are called therapists or counselors!

It's Okay to Talk About Feelings!

This is a very important message for everyone. If you ever feel very sad, or worried, or scared, it's super important to talk to a grown-up you trust. This could be your mum, dad, a teacher, a grandparent, or another kind grown-up. Talking about your feelings is like letting a little bit of sunshine into a cloudy day. It helps. It's brave to share how you feel. And remember, you are never alone with your feelings. Many people care about you. If you know a friend who seems very sad or quiet, you can be kind to them. You can ask them if they are okay. You can tell a grown-up that your friend seems sad. Being a good friend means looking out for each other.

How Police Help Us

In Zanzibar, the police officers are working hard. The police are like grown-up helpers who keep us safe. When something sad or confusing happens, they try to understand it. They talk to people who might know what happened. They ask questions to get all the pieces of the puzzle. Ashly's friend, her partner, is helping the police by telling them what he knows. This is called being a "witness." When someone is a witness, they just tell the police what they saw or heard. They help the police learn the truth. This helps everyone understand what happened and learn from it. The police are there to protect people and make sure everyone in the community is safe. They help families when sad things happen. **Did you know?** Police officers wear special uniforms and often drive special cars with flashing lights! They help us every day.

Zanzibar: A Beautiful Place

Even with sad news, it's good to remember the beauty around us. Zanzibar is a truly wonderful place in Africa. It's famous for its beautiful white sand beaches and clear blue ocean. People from all over the world love to visit Zanzibar. They swim, play, and learn about its history. Zanzibar also grows yummy spices, like cloves and vanilla. This is why it's sometimes called the "Spice Island"! It reminds us that even when there's sadness, there's also so much beauty and kindness in the world. We can always look for the good things and help them grow. **Did you know?** Zanzibar is also home to special red monkeys called "Red Colobus Monkeys"! They are very cute and playful.

Let's Be Kind to Each Other

So, what can we learn from this news? We learn that sometimes people feel very, very sad. And when they do, they need our kindness and help. We learn that it's always okay to talk about our feelings, no matter how big or small they are. It's good to share them with a grown-up you trust. And we learn that by being kind, listening to our friends, and helping others, we can make our world a happier place for everyone. Let's promise to look out for each other, okay? Thank you for listening, my friends!
summary: A lady named Ashly Robinson who was very sad passed away in Zanzibar, and the police are helping to understand what happened, reminding us it's important to talk about our feelings.
whyMatters: This story is important because it teaches us to be kind, to look out for friends who might be sad, and always to talk to a grown-up if we feel big worries or sadness inside, because sharing helps.

originalTitle: The Take: Who is keeping the fight alive in Sudan’s war?
title: Let's Learn About Sudan and Hope for Peace!
content:
Hello, my amazing African friends! I'm your friendly news reporter, and today we're going to learn about a very special country in Africa called Sudan. It's a big country with lovely people, just like you! But for a little while, Sudan has been going through some big trouble. Let's talk about it in a simple way, and remember, we always hope for good things to happen.

Where is Sudan?

Sudan is a really big country in Africa. It's in the part of Africa that's often sunny and has deserts, but also a very famous river! Can you guess which one? It's the Nile River! This river is like a long, winding friend that brings water and life to many places. Sudan is a land of beautiful landscapes and wonderful people. Did you know? Sudan is sometimes called the 'land of the pyramids' because it actually has more ancient pyramids than Egypt! Imagine that! So many cool old buildings to see.

What's This 'War' We Hear About?

Sometimes, grown-ups in a country have a very, very big disagreement. Instead of just talking about it and finding a way to be friends, they start to fight. This big fight, when it goes on for a long time and makes many people sad and scared, is what we call a 'war'. It's not like the small fights you might have with your brother or sister; it's much bigger and more serious. For Sudan, this big trouble has been happening for three whole years. Imagine three years! That's a very long time for boys and girls to live with worry instead of just playing and learning. It means that everyday life, which should be full of smiles and games, becomes very difficult for many families.

Homes and Schools in Trouble

When there is a big fight, many things that help us live happily can get broken or unsafe. Imagine your cozy home, where you feel safe and warm. For many children in Sudan, their homes have become unsafe, or they've had to leave them behind to find a safer place. This means they can't sleep in their own beds or play in their own gardens anymore. And what about school? We all love going to school to learn new things, draw pictures, and play with our friends. But when there's a big fight, schools sometimes have to close. This is very sad because children miss out on learning their ABCs, counting numbers, and hearing exciting stories from their teachers. Education is so important, and many kids in Sudan haven't been able to go to school for a long time. They want to learn, just like you! Did you know? Sudan is home to many different groups of people, and they speak over 100 different languages! Isn't that amazing? It shows how rich and diverse the country is, with so many unique voices and stories.

Finding Food and Water Becomes Hard

When there's trouble, even getting simple things like food and clean water can become very hard. Imagine if your mom or dad couldn't go to the market to buy yummy food, or if the water tap stopped working. This is what happens to many families in Sudan. They might not have enough to eat or drink, which makes them feel weak and unwell. It's a very difficult situation for everyone, especially the little ones. People sometimes have to walk very long distances to find help, or to get some food and water. It's a journey filled with challenges, and they are very brave to keep going. We should think about them and hope they get the help they need very soon.

Friends and Family Apart

One of the saddest things about big fights is that they can separate families and friends. When homes are not safe, people have to move to different places. This means children might be far away from their grandparents, cousins, or best friends. They miss playing together, sharing secrets, and giving big hugs. It's a lonely feeling to be away from the people you love. But even when they are apart, people try to stay in touch and help each other. They remember their homes and hope for a day when they can all be together again, safe and sound. It's a strong wish that many people hold in their hearts.

Brave People Are Helping!

Even though there's a big fight, many brave and kind people are working very hard to help. These people are like real-life superheroes! They are called aid workers or helpers. They bring food, clean water, and medicine to those who need it most. They set up safe places where children can play and learn, even if it's not a real school building. They travel to dangerous places to make sure that families get the help they need. These helpers show us that even in tough times, there is always kindness and hope. Their work is super important because it brings a little bit of light into a dark situation. We should be very thankful for their courage and their big hearts. Did you know? The capital city of Sudan is called Khartoum! It's where the two main parts of the Nile River meet – the Blue Nile and the White Nile! It’s like a watery hug right in the middle of the city.

What Can We Do?

As kids, you might wonder what you can do. Well, the most important thing is to remember the children and families in Sudan. You can talk to your parents about them, draw pictures of peace, and send your good thoughts and prayers. Every little bit of kindness and hope helps. You can also learn more about Sudan and its beautiful culture, so when peace comes, you know how wonderful it is. We can all hope for a day when the big fight stops, and peace returns to Sudan. A day when children can go back to school, play safely in their homes, and be reunited with their friends and families. A day when there is plenty of food and clean water for everyone. Let's all wish for a bright and peaceful future for Sudan. Remember, even small voices can spread big hope! Thank you for listening, my dear friends.
summary: For three long years, the beautiful country of Sudan has faced big troubles, making it hard for children to go to school, families to stay safe, and everyone to get food, but many brave helpers are bringing hope for peace.
whyMatters: It's important to know about Sudan because children and families there are going through a very hard time, and we can all send them our good wishes and hope for peace to return soon so they can live happily.
